goal: Land the G2 intra-group Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF) algorithm in a NEW `crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` module — pure data-structure / arithmetic with the canonical RRF formula `score(d) = Σ_r weight(r) / (k + rank_r(d))` per master-plan §5.2 line 457, weights frozen at Probe×2.0, ripgrep×1.5, LanceDB×1.5 (Zoekt 1.0 / codedb 1.0 also seated to keep the `match` exhaustive when the wider source-set lands in Phase 3), `k = 60` per §6.2 line 645, deterministic `(file_path, start_line, end_line)` grouping via `BTreeMap` (per WO-0048 lessons line 333 — eliminates an end-of-pass sort), output sorted descending by `fused_score` so the top-ranked hit is `[0]`, with `per_source_ranks` provenance preserved on every fused hit so a future search_code MCP layer (P2-W7-F06) can surface 'which engines found this'.
plan_summary: Add `crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` (NEW module) declaring `pub enum G2Source { Ripgrep, Probe, Lancedb, Zoekt, Codedb }` (5 variants per master-plan §5.2 line 457), `pub struct G2Hit { file_path, start_line, end_line, snippet, score }`, `pub struct G2SourceResults { source, hits }`, `pub struct G2FusedHit { file_path, start_line, end_line, snippet, fused_score, contributing_sources, per_source_ranks }`, `pub struct G2FusedOutcome { hits }`. Add `pub const G2_RRF_K: u32 = 60;` and `pub const fn rrf_weight(source: G2Source) -> f64` returning `match { Ripgrep => 1.5, Probe => 2.0, Lancedb => 1.5, Zoekt => 1.0, Codedb => 1.0 }` (`const fn` per WO-0048 lessons line 354 `clippy::missing_const_for_fn`). Implement `pub fn fuse_g2_rrf(per_source: &[G2SourceResults]) -> G2FusedOutcome` that (a) groups hits by `(PathBuf, u32, u32)` location key into a `BTreeMap`, (b) for each location sums `weight × 1.0/(k as f64 + rank as f64)` over contributing sources (rank is 1-based position in that source's `hits` slice), (c) selects the snippet from the highest-weight contributing source (ties broken by enum-discriminant order — matches the WO-0048 `authority_rank` pattern), (d) sorts contributing_sources by descending weight then by enum-discriminant for tie-breaking, (e) sorts the final hits Vec descending by `fused_score` (NaN-safe via `partial_cmp` fallback to `Equal`). Add `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.group.search.fusion", level = "debug", skip(per_source), fields(input_sources = per_source.len()))]` per master-plan §15.2 span naming. Frozen acceptance test `#[test] fn test_g2_rrf_weights()` at MODULE ROOT of fusion.rs (per DEC-0007), 4-source scenario (3 in-scope + verify Zoekt/Codedb arms compile via the const-fn weight match), 7 sub-assertions covering: (1) hits.len() == 4, (2) constants `G2_RRF_K == 60` + 5 `rrf_weight` arms, (3) top-ranked hit is `(foo.rs, 10, 20)` with Probe+Ripgrep contributors, (4) Probe×2.0 weight wins over Lancedb×1.5 at equal rank (qux.rs:1-3 outranks foo.rs:30-40), (5) per_source_ranks populated with `[(Probe, 1), (Ripgrep, 1)]` for the top hit, (6) deterministic descending sort by fused_score, (7) contributing_sources for the top hit ordered `[Probe, Ripgrep]` (highest-weight first). Update `crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs` adding `pub mod fusion;` plus a single `pub use fusion::{...}` re-export line covering all 7 new public symbols (`G2_RRF_K`, `G2FusedHit`, `G2FusedOutcome`, `G2Hit`, `G2Source`, `G2SourceResults`, `fuse_g2_rrf`, `rrf_weight`) per the WO-0042 → WO-0048 cumulative-debt-avoidance discipline (now 7 consecutive WOs cleared). Add `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F03.sh` per the standing convention. Pre-bake 3 mutation entries in `acceptance` (literal sed AND runtime-only variant per WO-0046 lessons line 245): (#1) `fuse_g2_rrf` body neutered to `G2FusedOutcome::default()` → AC05 sub-assertion 1 panics on observed-length 0; (#2) `G2_RRF_K` literal swapped from `60` to `1` → AC09 sub-assertion 4 fails because the score values change dramatically and direct `assert_eq!(G2_RRF_K, 60)` panics; (#3) `rrf_weight` body collapsed to `1.0` for all variants → AC11 sub-assertion 4 fails because Probe×2.0 vs Lancedb×1.5 differentiation collapses, qux.rs:1-3 no longer outranks foo.rs:30-40.
scope_in: Create NEW file `crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` declaring `pub enum G2Source { Ripgrep, Probe, Lancedb, Zoekt, Codedb }` with `Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq, Hash, PartialOrd, Ord, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize` derives. The 5 variants match master-plan §5.2 line 457 exactly. `Ord` derive makes the variant enum-discriminant ordering deterministic for tie-breaking. Cite §5.2 line 457 in the rustdoc.

Create `pub struct G2Hit { pub file_path: PathBuf, pub start_line: u32, pub end_line: u32, pub snippet: String, pub score: f64 }` with `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize` derives (NOT `Eq` — `f64` is not `Eq`). Lines are 1-based. `start_line == end_line` is permitted. `snippet` is the per-source rendered text excerpt. `score` is the per-source raw score used by that source's own ranking (NOT the fused score — that lives on `G2FusedHit`).

Create `pub struct G2SourceResults { pub source: G2Source, pub hits: Vec<G2Hit> }` with `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Default, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize` derives. The `hits` Vec is the source's ALREADY-RANKED output — `hits[0]` is rank 1, `hits[1]` is rank 2, etc. (1-based ranks consumed by the RRF formula). `Default` derive enables empty-source test inputs.

Create `pub struct G2FusedHit { pub file_path: PathBuf, pub start_line: u32, pub end_line: u32, pub snippet: String, pub fused_score: f64, pub contributing_sources: Vec<G2Source>, pub per_source_ranks: Vec<(G2Source, u32)> }` with `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Default, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize` derives (NOT `Eq` — `f64` is not `Eq`). `contributing_sources` is sorted by descending `rrf_weight` then ascending enum-discriminant for ties, so a reader can spot the highest-weight contributor at index 0. `per_source_ranks` is `(source, rank)` pairs preserving the per-source rank that the source assigned to this location — provenance for the future P2-W7-F06 `search_code` MCP-tool layer to surface 'Probe ranked this #1, ripgrep ranked this #3'. `Default` derive enables the runtime-only mutation variant for AC18.

Create `pub struct G2FusedOutcome { pub hits: Vec<G2FusedHit> }` with `Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Default, serde::Serialize, serde::Deserialize` derives. `hits` is sorted descending by `fused_score` so `hits[0]` is the top result. `Default` derive (empty `hits` Vec) enables the runtime-only mutation variant for AC17.

Add `pub const G2_RRF_K: u32 = 60;` per master-plan §5.2 line 457 and §6.2 line 645 ('k tunable, default 60'). The const is `u32` (NOT `usize`) so the `f64` cast in the RRF formula `(k as f64 + rank as f64)` is unambiguous on 32-bit and 64-bit targets identically. Cite §6.2 line 645 in the rustdoc.

Add `pub const fn rrf_weight(source: G2Source) -> f64` returning the master-plan §5.2 line 457 weights via an exhaustive `match`: `Ripgrep => 1.5, Probe => 2.0, Lancedb => 1.5, Zoekt => 1.0, Codedb => 1.0`. `const fn` per WO-0048 lessons line 354 `clippy::missing_const_for_fn`. The exhaustive `match` ensures any future variant addition fails to compile until `rrf_weight` is updated — same compiler-enforcement pattern as WO-0048's `authority_rank` (`crates/ucil-daemon/src/executor.rs:1300`).

Implement `pub fn fuse_g2_rrf(per_source: &[G2SourceResults]) -> G2FusedOutcome` with this exact contract: (a) Build a `BTreeMap<(PathBuf, u32, u32), Vec<(G2Source, u32, &G2Hit)>>` mapping each location key to the list of `(source, rank, hit_ref)` tuples that contributed to it. Iterate `per_source.iter()` once; for each source, enumerate `hits.iter().enumerate()` and use `idx + 1` as the 1-based rank. Use `&G2Hit` borrows here — the data lives in `per_source` for the duration of the call so the borrow is sound. (b) For each `((file_path, start_line, end_line), contributors)` in the BTreeMap, compute the fused hit: (i) `fused_score = contributors.iter().map(|(src, rank, _)| rrf_weight(*src) * (1.0 / (G2_RRF_K as f64 + *rank as f64))).sum::<f64>()`. (ii) `per_source_ranks = contributors.iter().map(|(src, rank, _)| (*src, *rank)).collect::<Vec<_>>()` — preserved in input encounter order. (iii) `contributing_sources = contributors.iter().map(|(src, _, _)| *src).collect::<Vec<_>>()` then sort by `(Reverse(rrf_weight(s).to_bits()), s)` — descending weight, ascending enum-discriminant for tie-breaking. (`f64::to_bits` on positive non-NaN values is monotone-equivalent to `f64` ordering — safe here because `rrf_weight` only emits {1.0, 1.5, 2.0}.) (iv) `snippet` = the snippet from the highest-weight contributing source (i.e. the source at `contributing_sources[0]`); locate the matching `&G2Hit` via the contributors list and `.snippet.clone()`. (c) Build `hits: Vec<G2FusedHit>` from the BTreeMap iteration. (d) Sort `hits` descending by `fused_score` using `sort_by(|a, b| b.fused_score.partial_cmp(&a.fused_score).unwrap_or(std::cmp::Ordering::Equal))` — NaN-safe (no NaN expected since RRF over positive weights / positive denominators is always positive-finite, but the fallback keeps `partial_cmp`'s `Option<Ordering>` from forcing a panic). Tie-break on equal `fused_score` by `(file_path, start_line, end_line)` ascending so the output is deterministic. (e) Return `G2FusedOutcome { hits }`. The function MUST NOT panic, MUST NOT return `Result` (RRF over a possibly-empty slice is just `G2FusedOutcome { hits: vec![] }`), and MUST NOT use `.unwrap()` / `.expect()` outside `#[cfg(test)]`.

Add `#[tracing::instrument(name = "ucil.group.search.fusion", level = "debug", skip(per_source), fields(input_sources = per_source.len(), input_hits = per_source.iter().map(|r| r.hits.len()).sum::<usize>()))]` on `fuse_g2_rrf` per master-plan §15.2 span naming `ucil.<layer>.<op>` (this is the G2 search layer's fusion operation; the symmetric G1 fusion span landed in WO-0048 as `ucil.group.structural.fusion`).

Add the frozen acceptance test `#[test] fn test_g2_rrf_weights()` at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. Sync `#[test]` (NOT `#[tokio::test]`) — RRF is pure CPU math, no async/IO. The test must construct THIS exact 3-source scenario:  
  - Source #1 = Ripgrep with 3 hits ranked: rank 1 (`foo.rs`, 10, 20, snippet `"fn foo() // ripgrep"`, score 0.8), rank 2 (`foo.rs`, 30, 40, snippet `"fn bar() // ripgrep"`, score 0.7), rank 3 (`baz.rs`, 5, 10, snippet `"fn baz() // ripgrep"`, score 0.6).  
  - Source #2 = Probe with 2 hits ranked: rank 1 (`foo.rs`, 10, 20, snippet `"fn foo() // probe"`, score 0.95), rank 2 (`qux.rs`, 1, 3, snippet `"fn qux() // probe"`, score 0.5).  
  - Source #3 = Lancedb with 2 hits ranked: rank 1 (`foo.rs`, 30, 40, snippet `"fn bar() // lancedb"`, score 0.9), rank 2 (`qux.rs`, 1, 3, snippet `"fn qux() // lancedb"`, score 0.7).  
  Call `let outcome = fuse_g2_rrf(&[ripgrep, probe, lancedb]);` and assert ALL of:  
  (a) **Sub-assertion 1 — location grouping**: `outcome.hits.len() == 4` (locations `(foo.rs, 10, 20)`, `(foo.rs, 30, 40)`, `(baz.rs, 5, 10)`, `(qux.rs, 1, 3)`).  
  (b) **Sub-assertion 2 — top-ranked hit**: `outcome.hits[0].file_path.ends_with("foo.rs")` AND `outcome.hits[0].start_line == 10` AND `outcome.hits[0].end_line == 20`. Computed score: `2.0/(60+1) + 1.5/(60+1) ≈ 0.05738` — assert `outcome.hits[0].fused_score > 0.057 && outcome.hits[0].fused_score < 0.058` (range bounded so a small floating-point drift in compiler optimisation does not produce a false fail).  
  (c) **Sub-assertion 3 — Probe×2.0 dominance**: `outcome.hits[1]` is `(qux.rs, 1, 3)` and `outcome.hits[2]` is `(foo.rs, 30, 40)` — i.e. qux.rs:1-3 (Probe rank 2 + Lancedb rank 2 = 2.0/62 + 1.5/62 ≈ 0.0565) outranks foo.rs:30-40 (Ripgrep rank 2 + Lancedb rank 1 = 1.5/62 + 1.5/61 ≈ 0.0488). This is THE load-bearing assertion — if Probe's 2.0 weight is silently changed to 1.5 (or to anything ≤ Lancedb's 1.5), the ordering reverses. Quote actual values in the panic message.  
  (d) **Sub-assertion 4 — constants**: `assert_eq!(G2_RRF_K, 60);` AND `assert_eq!(rrf_weight(G2Source::Probe), 2.0);` AND `assert_eq!(rrf_weight(G2Source::Ripgrep), 1.5);` AND `assert_eq!(rrf_weight(G2Source::Lancedb), 1.5);` AND `assert_eq!(rrf_weight(G2Source::Zoekt), 1.0);` AND `assert_eq!(rrf_weight(G2Source::Codedb), 1.0);`.  
  (e) **Sub-assertion 5 — per_source_ranks provenance**: `outcome.hits[0].per_source_ranks` (the foo.rs:10-20 entry) contains exactly `[(G2Source::Ripgrep, 1), (G2Source::Probe, 1)]` (or `[(Probe, 1), (Ripgrep, 1)]` — the input encounter order matches the per_source slice order). Verify via `assert_eq!` against a sorted copy of the actual against a sorted expected, so the assertion is order-insensitive on per_source_ranks (each contributing source appears exactly once with its assigned rank).  
  (f) **Sub-assertion 6 — descending sort invariant**: `for w in outcome.hits.windows(2) { assert!(w[0].fused_score >= w[1].fused_score, "hits not sorted desc by fused_score: {:?}", outcome.hits); }`.  
  (g) **Sub-assertion 7 — contributing_sources highest-weight-first**: `outcome.hits[0].contributing_sources[0] == G2Source::Probe` (Probe's 2.0 weight beats Ripgrep's 1.5 at the (foo.rs, 10, 20) location) AND `outcome.hits[0].contributing_sources[1] == G2Source::Ripgrep`.  
  All assertions use `assert_eq!` / `assert!` with operator-readable panic messages quoting the actual `outcome` Debug-print on failure (per WO-0042 lessons line 92 — `matches!()` is NOT permitted for anything that can be expressed as `assert_eq!`).

Update `crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs` adding `pub mod fusion;` (alphabetical insertion BETWEEN `pub mod incremental;` line 10 and `pub mod knowledge_graph;` line 11 — i.e. position the new `pub mod` line between `incremental` and `knowledge_graph` alphabetically). Then add a NEW re-export block immediately after the existing `pub use incremental::{...}` line (line 21) and BEFORE the `pub use knowledge_graph::SymbolResolution;` block (line 27): `pub use fusion::{fuse_g2_rrf, rrf_weight, G2FusedHit, G2FusedOutcome, G2Hit, G2Source, G2SourceResults, G2_RRF_K};` — single line listing all 8 new public symbols in alphabetical order (functions first by the existing convention `dependent_metric, symbol_count, FileRevision`-style, then types, then consts; here all three categories on one line is acceptable since rustfmt will not split a 100-col line that fits). Verify post-edit via `grep -nE 'fuse_g2_rrf|rrf_weight|G2FusedHit|G2FusedOutcome|G2Hit|G2Source|G2SourceResults|G2_RRF_K' crates/ucil-core/src/lib.rs` returning all 8 symbols on the new line. Do NOT touch the existing `pub use knowledge_graph::*` / `pub use otel::*` / `pub use schema_migration::*` / `pub use types::*` lines.

Add `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F03.sh` per the standing convention (model on `scripts/verify/P2-W7-F02.sh`). Script must (a) run `cargo test -p ucil-core fusion::test_g2_rrf_weights -- --nocapture` and tee its stdout to a tmpfile, (b) match either `cargo test`'s `test result: ok. ... 0 failed` shape OR nextest's `... tests passed` shape via the tolerant alternation `grep -Eq 'test result: ok\. .* 0 failed|[0-9]+ tests? passed'` (per WO-0048 / WO-0042 lessons — same reusable shape), (c) emit `[FAIL] P2-W7-F03: <reason>` on non-zero exit with a hint pointing at the assertion line, (d) `chmod +x scripts/verify/P2-W7-F03.sh` so the verifier can run it directly. Header comment block cites WO-0050 + P2-W7-F03 + the `cargo test` selector verbatim.

**Anti-cascade for `#![deny(warnings)]`**: per-mutation patches MUST use the runtime-only variant pattern from WO-0046 lessons line 245 — `let _ = arg;` for any arg the mutated body discards, plus a no-op return of `G2FusedOutcome::default()` (the type derives `Default`, enabling this). The `unreachable_code` warning on the rest of the body MAY require `#[allow(unreachable_code, clippy::needless_return, clippy::let_unit_value)]` on the whole function for the mutation step. Document BOTH the literal sed AND the runtime-only variant in `acceptance` mutation entries (AC17, AC18, AC19) — verifier picks whichever produces the runtime-assertion failure rather than a compile-failure cascade.

**Rustdoc on every public symbol** with backticks on every uppercase identifier per WO-0043 lessons line 128 `clippy::doc_markdown`. Pre-flight grep BEFORE pushing: `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` returns zero hits OR every match is inside backticks (per AC02). `# Examples` rustdoc block on `fuse_g2_rrf` showing a 2-source case (Ripgrep + Probe both hit `(foo.rs, 10, 20)` at rank 1; result has `fused_score ≈ 0.0574`, `contributing_sources = [Probe, Ripgrep]`). Use `no_run` or full-doctest depending on whether the example calls async (here it does not, so a full doctest with `assert!` is OK). Cite master-plan §5.2 line 457 + §6.2 line 645 in the module-level `//!` preamble.

**No production wiring of real ripgrep / Probe / LanceDB clients.** F03 is pure data-structure / arithmetic. The 3 in-scope sources (Probe / ripgrep / LanceDB) and 2 reserved sources (Zoekt / codedb) are seated in the enum and `rrf_weight` for compile-time exhaustiveness, but no subprocess call, no `tokio::spawn`, no `tokio::time::timeout`, no MCP-server wiring lives in this WO. The future `search_code` MCP tool (P2-W7-F06) is the natural consumer that wires real ripgrep (already in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/text_search.rs` per DEC-0009), real Probe (via the WO-0044 plugin manifest `plugins/search/probe/plugin.toml`), and real LanceDB (P2-W7-F09 + P2-W8-F04) into `G2SourceResults` inputs. Cite §5.2 line 457 + this deferral in the lib.rs preamble paragraph (see scope_in[10]).
scope_out: **No production wiring** of real ripgrep, Probe, LanceDB, Zoekt, or codedb clients. F03 ships the RRF math + 1 unit test in fusion.rs ONLY. Production wiring is deferred to P2-W7-F06 (`search_code` MCP tool) per master-plan §18 Phase 2 Week 7 line 1781-1783.

**No Jaccard-overlap deduplication** of overlapping line ranges (master-plan §5.2 line 458 'overlapping line ranges (Jaccard > 0.7) merged'). F03's grouping is exact-location (`(file_path, start_line, end_line)` tuple equality). Jaccard merge is a separate Phase-2 / Phase-3 surface; the F03 contract is RRF-on-exact-location only.

**No session deduplication.** Master-plan §5.2 line 459 ('Session dedup: don't return same code block twice in a session') is feature P2-W7-F04, a sibling WO. Do NOT touch `crates/ucil-daemon/src/session_manager.rs` — it is in `forbidden_paths`.

**No MCP-tool wiring.** The `search_code` MCP tool currently lives in `crates/ucil-daemon/src/server.rs` (Phase 1 stub at `_meta.not_yet_implemented: false` with the WO-0035 in-process ripgrep landing per DEC-0009) and is the F06 target. F03 does NOT add `find_definition` / `find_references` / `search_code` / any MCP tool wiring. `crates/ucil-daemon/**` is in `forbidden_paths`.

**No new dependencies.** `serde_json` is workspace, `serde` is workspace, `tracing` is workspace, `std::collections::BTreeMap` is std, `std::path::PathBuf` is std. `Cargo.toml` files MUST stay unchanged — AC15 verifies `git diff --name-only main...HEAD -- '*.toml'` returns empty. The 4 transient `serde_json` references in scope are the ones already pulled into `crates/ucil-core/Cargo.toml:14`.

**No new ADR.** DEC-0007 (frozen-selector module-root placement), DEC-0005 (module-coherence commits up to ~200 LOC), DEC-0009 (in-process ripgrep is the existing G2 substrate — F03 only fuses, doesn't shell out), DEC-0010 (`tests/integration/` workspace crate — not used by this WO since the test lives at module root in `fusion.rs`) all cover the WO. If a NEW pattern surfaces (e.g. `f64`-Hash collision in BTreeMap key, surprising serde-derive interactions on `G2FusedHit`), STOP and write an ADR before working around.

**No `cargo-mutants` per-WO gate.** DEC-0007 removed it; pre-baked function-body mutations in `acceptance` are the authoritative anti-laziness layer (carry from WO-0042 / 0043 / 0044 / 0045 / 0046 / 0047 / 0048, now 7 consecutive cleared).

**No agent narrative / synthesis.** Master-plan §5.2 line 460 names the output as 'ranked results with provenance (which engines found each result)' — this is exactly `G2FusedHit.contributing_sources` + `per_source_ranks`. Agent-narrative synthesis (Phase 3 / agent-system feature) is NOT in scope.

**No changes to `crates/ucil-daemon/**`, `crates/ucil-cli/**`, `crates/ucil-treesitter/**`, `crates/ucil-lsp-diagnostics/**`, `crates/ucil-embeddings/**`, `crates/ucil-agents/**`, `adapters/**`, `ml/**`, `plugins/**`, `tests/**`** — F03 is single-crate (`ucil-core`), single-source-file (NEW `fusion.rs`) plus the lib.rs re-exports + the verify script. Same shape as WO-0044 (plugin manifests, zero-source-change daemon) but applied to ucil-core.
acceptance: AC01 — `cargo build -p ucil-core` exits 0.

AC02 — `cargo clippy -p ucil-core --all-targets -- -D warnings` exits 0. Pre-flight grep `rg -nE '^\s*///.*\b[A-Z][A-Z_0-9]+\b' crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` returns zero hits OR every match is inside backticks (per WO-0043 lessons line 128 `clippy::doc_markdown`).
AC03 — `cargo test -p ucil-core fusion::test_g2_rrf_weights -- --nocapture` exits 0.

AC04 — Test selector `fusion::test_g2_rrf_weights` lives at MODULE ROOT of `crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` (NOT inside `mod tests {}`) per DEC-0007. Verifier checks via `grep -nE '^fn test_g2_rrf_weights|^pub fn test_g2_rrf_weights' crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` returning a non-`mod tests {}`-nested line. The matching `#[test]` attribute appears on the line immediately above the `fn` line.

AC05 — Sub-assertion 1 (location grouping): `outcome.hits.len() == 4` for the 3-source scenario in scope_in[9]. Panic message MUST quote `outcome` Debug-print on failure for operator-actionable diagnostics.

AC06 — Sub-assertion 2 (top-ranked hit): `outcome.hits[0].file_path.ends_with("foo.rs") && outcome.hits[0].start_line == 10 && outcome.hits[0].end_line == 20`. `outcome.hits[0].fused_score > 0.057 && outcome.hits[0].fused_score < 0.058` (range-bounded on the computed `2.0/61 + 1.5/61 ≈ 0.05738` to absorb compiler floating-point variation).

AC07 — Sub-assertion 3 (Probe×2.0 dominance — load-bearing): `outcome.hits[1]` corresponds to `(qux.rs, 1, 3)` AND `outcome.hits[2]` corresponds to `(foo.rs, 30, 40)`. THE assertion that fails when Probe's 2.0 weight is silently changed: if Probe is anything ≤ Lancedb's 1.5, qux.rs:1-3 vs foo.rs:30-40 ordering reverses. Verifier MUST quote both fused_score values in the panic message.

AC08 — Sub-assertion 4 (constants): `assert_eq!(G2_RRF_K, 60)` AND 5 weight asserts: `rrf_weight(G2Source::Probe) == 2.0`, `rrf_weight(G2Source::Ripgrep) == 1.5`, `rrf_weight(G2Source::Lancedb) == 1.5`, `rrf_weight(G2Source::Zoekt) == 1.0`, `rrf_weight(G2Source::Codedb) == 1.0`. Direct constant assertion is the cleanest catch for the AC18 K-mutation.

AC09 — Sub-assertion 5 (per_source_ranks provenance): `outcome.hits[0].per_source_ranks` (the (foo.rs, 10, 20) entry) contains exactly the 2-element multiset `{(G2Source::Ripgrep, 1), (G2Source::Probe, 1)}`. Verify via sort-then-equal so the assertion is order-insensitive on the multiset.

AC10 — Sub-assertion 6 (descending sort invariant): `for w in outcome.hits.windows(2) { assert!(w[0].fused_score >= w[1].fused_score, ...); }` panics with the full `outcome.hits` Debug-print if the invariant breaks. Catches a regression where the final `sort_by` is removed or has its `partial_cmp` direction reversed.

AC11 — Sub-assertion 7 (contributing_sources highest-weight-first): `outcome.hits[0].contributing_sources[0] == G2Source::Probe` AND `outcome.hits[0].contributing_sources[1] == G2Source::Ripgrep`. Catches a regression where the within-hit source ordering is left as input order (Ripgrep would come first because it appears first in `per_source`).

AC17 — Pre-baked mutation #1 (fusion body neutered):  
  Literal sed: `sed -i 's|pub fn fuse_g2_rrf(per_source: \&\[G2SourceResults\]) -> G2FusedOutcome {|pub fn fuse_g2_rrf(per_source: \&[G2SourceResults]) -> G2FusedOutcome { let _ = per_source; return G2FusedOutcome::default();|' crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` (prepends an early-return that discards the input).  
  Runtime-only variant (use this if literal sed produces a `#![deny(warnings)]` cascade per WO-0046 lessons line 245): replace the `fuse_g2_rrf` body's first executable statement with `let _ = per_source; return G2FusedOutcome::default();` (drops any `mut` bindings, discards the arg with `let _ = X;`, returns `Default`). The `unreachable_code` warning on the rest of the body MAY require `#[allow(unreachable_code, clippy::needless_return, clippy::let_unit_value)]` on the whole function for the mutation step.  
  Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs`. Rerun: green.  
  Expected runtime failure on `cargo test -p ucil-core fusion::test_g2_rrf_weights`: panic at AC05 sub-assertion 1 (`outcome.hits.len() == 4` fails because `Default::default()` produces an empty `hits` Vec — observed length 0).  
  `G2FusedOutcome` MUST `derive Default` for this mutation patch to compile — bake that derive into the type definition (per scope_in[4]).

AC18 — Pre-baked mutation #2 (RRF k constant swap):  
  Literal sed: `sed -i 's|pub const G2_RRF_K: u32 = 60;|pub const G2_RRF_K: u32 = 1;|' crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` (swaps `60` to `1` — denominators collapse from `61..63` to `2..4` and scores blow up by ~30×; AC08's `assert_eq!(G2_RRF_K, 60)` panics directly).  
  Runtime-only variant: NOT NEEDED — a `pub const` swap does NOT trigger any `#![deny(warnings)]` cascade (the constant has no body to mutate, just the literal). The literal sed is the only patch shape.  
  Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs`. Rerun: green.  
  Expected runtime failure on `cargo test -p ucil-core fusion::test_g2_rrf_weights`: panic at AC08 sub-assertion 4 (`assert_eq!(G2_RRF_K, 60)` — observed value 1). The K-mutation is the cleanest single-literal mutation in the WO; the constant assertion catches it directly.

AC19 — Pre-baked mutation #3 (rrf_weight differentiation collapsed):  
  Literal sed: `sed -i 's|G2Source::Probe => 2.0|G2Source::Probe => 1.0|' crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` (silently changes Probe's 2.0 to 1.0; the `match` is still exhaustive so it compiles, but the weight differentiation collapses — Probe×1.0 < Ripgrep×1.5).  
  Runtime-only variant (if the `match`-arm sed pattern is fragile due to formatting): replace the `match source` body inside `rrf_weight` with `match source { G2Source::Probe => 1.0, G2Source::Ripgrep => 1.5, G2Source::Lancedb => 1.5, G2Source::Zoekt => 1.0, G2Source::Codedb => 1.0 }` — same effect.  
  Restore: `git checkout -- crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs`. Rerun: green.  
  Expected runtime failure on `cargo test -p ucil-core fusion::test_g2_rrf_weights`: PRIMARY panic at AC08 sub-assertion 4 (`rrf_weight(G2Source::Probe) == 2.0` — observed 1.0). SECONDARY panic possible at AC07 sub-assertion 3 (Probe×1.0 weight no longer dominates Lancedb×1.5; qux.rs:1-3 score becomes 1.0/62 + 1.5/62 = 2.5/62 ≈ 0.0403, while foo.rs:30-40 stays at 1.5/62 + 1.5/61 ≈ 0.0488 → foo.rs:30-40 now outranks qux.rs:1-3, AC07's hit-position assertion fails). EITHER panic catches the regression (per WO-0048 lessons line 359 'when the WO's predicted fail line and the empirical fail line diverge, both fail-modes detect the regression — verifier cites both and PASSes the mutation, does NOT block on the predicted-line discrepancy').

AC20 — Stub-scan: `rg -nE 'todo!\(\)|unimplemented!\(\)|panic!\(".*not yet|TODO|FIXME' crates/ucil-core/src/fusion.rs` returns ZERO hits (this is a NEW file so the comparison is against zero, not against `git show main:...`). `unwrap()` is permitted ONLY inside `#[cfg(test)]` per root CLAUDE.md (the test fn at module root uses sync `#[test]` with no `unwrap` paths; if the executor adds any, it MUST be inside `#[cfg(test)]` blocks ONLY).
